prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Friday lunchtime: 10, Monday morning: 10, Saturday night: 10, Sunday night: 10, Wednesday evening: 10. You have been paired with another LLM. If you choose the same object as your partner, you will both receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, neither will receive anything. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

responses:
mercury the sun is the center of our solar system making it uniquely central

earth its the planet we live on and has the most features compared to other planets

mars its the red planet and has the highest number of moons

venus its the second planet from the sun and has the densest atmosphere

saturn its the eighth planet from the sun and has the largest moon family

these objects are unique because they are the only ones with their own sun the only planet with its own atmosphere and the only planet with its own moons they also have the highest number of natural satellites making them extremely significant within their respective planetary systems therefore mercury is the only object that meets these criteria

mercury the sun is the only planet with a clear surface and liquid water making it a unique and essential element for life on earth it is also the closest planet to the sun making it extremely close to us and potentially having significant effects on our planets climate and environment however mercury does not have any other known natural resources or phenomena that would justify its being considered an extreme object especially when compared to the vastness of space and the vastness of the universe itself
mercury the planet closest to the sun and most similar to earth making it the only planet with a similarity property
